findings.justification: actionIndex is reachable via wp_ajax_smart-slider3 (any authenticated WordPress user) and only validates a nonce (validateToken), never calling current_user_can() or validatePermission(), so any logged-in user can enumerate the server's image directory tree.

SVG files (image/svg+xml) are accepted, moved to disk without any content sanitization, and served back via URL — an attacker with upload_files capability can upload an SVG containing embedded JavaScript that executes in victim browsers when the file URL is visited.

When the resolved path falls outside the images root, both handlers re-allow the original (unresolved) path if any ancestor is a symlink, enabling a server-side symlink planted in the uploads tree to expose or receive files outside the intended root.
The AJAX response includes the key 'fullPath' containing the absolute server filesystem path, disclosing the installation layout to any authenticated user who can reach this endpoint.
